transitions.notes:
Upper layer requests disconnect. Tear down: discard pending I
frames, reset retry counter, send DISC with P=1, swap timers
(stop T3, start T1 for the DISC ack wait), → AwaitingRelease.

references.quote: While in the information-transfer state, either TNC may indicate a request to disconnect the link by transmitting a DISC command frame and starting timer T1.

references.note: sets DISCPENDING if TX_Q non-empty; immediate state 4 + T1=1 otherwise — figure says always discard + DISC(P=1)
state_3/state_4 fall-through: discard queue, DISC cmd P=1, STOP_T3 / START_T1, → state_2
fused Connected+TimerRecovery; clears iframe queue, rc=0, sends DISC, → AwaitingRelease. Author TODO flag on timer choice.
STATE_3/STATE_4 branch — Linux collapses Connected and TimerRecovery into one DISC path

transitions.notes:
I frame received as a response rather than a command — protocol
error. DL-ERROR(O), discard, stay.

references.note: in-sequence path; LinBPQ funnels all I-frame logic through SDIFRM → PROC_I_FRAME → no spec_prose-mapped command check
state_3/state_4 entry. States 3 & 4 share body — divergence from figure
Author rejects spec's DlError::O on non-command I: "not even remotely correct, since O means packet too big" — emits DlError::S instead
no separate command-check; non-I frames fall to default (silent drop)

transitions.guard: command and not info_field_valid and version_2_2
transitions.notes:
Bad I-frame info field (length > N1 or not octet-aligned), v2.2 peer.
Re-establish link via the v2.2 path.

references.note: bad-info-length path: DL-ERROR(O) message, establish_data_link, state→1 or 5 (modulo-128 author enhancement)
Payload>N1 → establish_data_link + DlError::O + AwaitingConnection. Author TODO: "should we discard it?"
Length<1 or >257 → silent ReleaseBuffer. No DL-ERROR(O), no Establish — diverges from figure

transitions.guard: command and not info_field_valid and not version_2_2
transitions.notes: Bad I-frame info field, v2.0 peer. Re-establish via v2.0 path.

references.note: same code path as t03 — direwolf has no v2.0/v2.2 split for bad info length
same as t03
same as t03 — LinBPQ has no v22/v20 distinction for info-field length

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and not V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and version_2_2
transitions.notes:
N(r) outside the V(a)..V(s) window — peer ACK is invalid. Recover
via re-establish on v2.2 path.

references.note: LinBPQ collapse: all N(r) errors → SDFRMR/FRMR state. No N(r) Error Recovery → AwaitingConnection22
is_good_nr false → nr_error_recovery, → state 1 or 5 (author enhancement: modulo==128 ? state_5 : state_1)
N(R) out of range → nr_error_recovery + transition AwaitingConnection
ax25_validate_nr fails → nr_error_recovery + STATE_1

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and not V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not version_2_2
transitions.notes: As t05 but v2.0 peer.

references.note: same path as t05 — no v22/v20 split
same as t05
same as t05
same as t05

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and own_receiver_busy and P_eq_1
transitions.notes:
Own receiver is busy — drop the I-frame's contents. Peer polled
(P=1) so we owe a status response: RR with F=1.

references.quote: If the TNC is in a busy condition, it ignores any received I frames without reporting this condition, other than repeating the indication of the busy condition.

references.note: own_receiver_busy && p==1: send RNR response. Author flags "should be unreachable because we currently don't have a way to set own_receiver_busy" — figure path effectively dead in direwolf
own_receiver_busy: discard iframe (implicit), optionally send RNR if polled. Author note "discord (implicit)" typo
OWN_RX_BUSY: discard, send enquiry_response only if P=1, no V(r) advance
no separate own-busy-discard inside SDIFRM — busy decided dynamically at response-send time (QCOUNT<20)

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and own_receiver_busy and not P_eq_1
transitions.notes: Own busy and not polled — just drop the frame, stay.

references.quote: If the TNC is in a busy condition, it ignores any received I frames without reporting this condition, other than repeating the indication of the busy condition.

references.note: own_receiver_busy with !P=1: implicit no-op. See t07 note about unreachable code.
same OWN_RX_BUSY branch; P=0 takes the no-response path

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and N_s_eq_V_r and not V_r_I_frame_stored and P_eq_1
transitions.notes:
In-sequence I frame received, no stored frame for V(r), peer
polled. Deliver data, advance V(r), respond RR(F=1).

references.quote: If a TNC receives a valid I frame...it accepts the received I frame, increments its receive state variable, and acts in one of the following manners: …the receiving TNC sends an RR frame with N(R) equal to V(R).

references.note: P=1 path: send RR response with F=1 after in-order delivery
After in-order delivery with poll: send RR F=1, clear acknowledge_pending
ns==vr with pf=1: send enquiry_response (RR/RNR F=1) instead of arming T2
P-bit set → SEND_RR_RESP(LINK, PFBIT) at 2708

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and N_s_eq_V_r and not V_r_I_frame_stored and not P_eq_1 and acknowledge_pending
transitions.notes: In-sequence data; not polled; ack already pending — let it batch.

references.quote: If there are no outstanding I frames, the receiving TNC sends an RR frame with N(R) equal to V(R). The receiving TNC may wait a small period of time before sending the RR frame to be sure additional I frames are not being transmitted.

references.note: P=0, ack_pending: no-op; will piggyback or T2-fire later
In-order, no poll: set acknowledge_pending. Comment "LM seize request (?)"
Sets L2ACKREQ = PORTT2 (delayed-ack) — equivalent to ack-pending; sent later via L2TimerProc:3533
ACK_PENDING set + T2 started when pf=0 — T2 is Linux's deferred-ack timer (figc4.4 LM-SEIZE-request analogue)

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and N_s_eq_V_r and not V_r_I_frame_stored and not P_eq_1 and not acknowledge_pending
transitions.notes:
In-sequence data; not polled; no ack pending — arrange to send
one: seize the medium and set ack-pending so we know to RR when
we get LM-SEIZE Confirm (see column 24).

references.quote: If there are no outstanding I frames, the receiving TNC sends an RR frame with N(R) equal to V(R). The receiving TNC may wait a small period of time before sending the RR frame…

references.note: !ack_pending branch: set ack_pending=1, lm_seize_request
same handler as t10 — ack_pending flag set but no concrete LM seize emitted
same path as t10 via T2 timer

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and reject_exception and P_eq_1

references.quote: When an I frame is received with a correct FCS, but its send sequence number N(S) does not match the current receiver's receive state variable, the frame is discarded.

references.note: srej_enable==srej_none path: set reject_exception, REJ response, F=P
reject_exception already set: discard frame; respond RR only if polled
L2STATE==6 (REJ Sent) drops out of REJ when in-sequence frame arrives

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and reject_exception and not P_eq_1

references.quote: When an I frame is received with a correct FCS, but its send sequence number N(S) does not match the current receiver's receive state variable, the frame is discarded.

references.note: REJ outstanding + P=0: no response, stay
same handler as t12

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and not reject_exception and srej_enabled and not srej_exception_gt_0 and not N_s_gt_V_r_plus_1

references.quote: When an I frame is received…the frame is retained. SREJ frames are sent with a receive sequence number equal to the value N(R) of the missing frame, and P=1 if an uncleared SREJ condition has not been previously established.

references.note: SREJ-enabled and is_ns_in_window. Author Erratum 3014: "AX.25 protocol spec did not handle SREJ very well. Based on X.25 section 2.4.6.4." Major divergence — direwolf follows X.25 rather than AX.25 SDL
Single-frame gap with SREJ enabled: sreject_exception++, ack_pending=false. TODO: SendSrej action not implemented
Out-of-sequence stored in RXFRAMES[NS]; SREJ vs REJ chosen in RR_OR_RNR:4230

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and not reject_exception and srej_enabled and not srej_exception_gt_0 and N_s_gt_V_r_plus_1

references.quote: SREJ frames are sent with a receive sequence number equal to the value N(R) of the missing frame, and P=1 if an uncleared SREJ condition has not been previously established.

references.note: X.25 2.4.6.4 (a) — N(S) out of window, discard, respond if P=1
p.ns != (vr+1) % modulus: SendRej path (gap > 1). Comment "Maybe a version of if in_range(p.ns)" TODO

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and not reject_exception and srej_enabled and srej_exception_gt_0

references.quote: If an SREJ condition is already pending, an SREJ will be sent with P=0.

references.note: same SREJ-in-window path as t14 with existing exception
sreject_exception > 0: increment + clear ack_pending. TODO: actual SendSrej not implemented

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and not N_s_eq_V_r and not reject_exception and not srej_enabled

references.quote: A REJ frame is sent with a receive sequence number equal to one higher than the last correctly received I frame if an uncleared N(S) sequence error condition has not been previously established.

references.note: SREJ disabled: set reject_exception, REJ response, F=P. Same as t12
SREJ disabled path: set reject_exception, SendRej. Author TODO: "should we maybe wait a bit with sending a REJ?"
L2OUTOFSEQ++; L2STATE=6 (REJ state). REJ sent later when L2ACKREQ expires
REJ F=pf RESPONSE on first out-of-sequence I, ACK_PENDING cleared. Linux: go-back-N only — no SREJ alternative

transitions.notes:
verification_pending: graphml label is 'Push on I Frame Queue
(note: word order?)' — Tom flagged uncertainty about whether the
spec text is "Push on I Frame Queue" vs "Push I Frame on Queue"
(or similar word order). Canonical verb chosen here is
push_on_I_frame_queue. Worth a spec re-read.

references.quote: Whenever a TNC has an I frame to transmit, it sends the I frame with the N(S) of the control field equal to its current send state variable V(S).

references.note: C_Q_ADD(&LINK->TX_Q, Msg). Multiple L4 paths (637/908/919/1846/1950/1980) all push to TX_Q
state_3/state_4 fall-through: append to I-frame queue, then lm_seize_request if within window. v1.5 change at 1502-1506
Appends to obuf — "deliberately doesn't preserve the application's frame boundaries" — then calls Data::flush()
SOCK_SEQPACKET path: ax25_output() → ax25_kick(). No explicit DL-DATA primitive

transitions.guard: not peer_receiver_busy and not V_s_eq_V_a_plus_k and T1_running

references.quote: After the I frame is sent, the send state variable is incremented by one. If timer T1 is not running, it is started. If timer T1 is running, it is restarted.

references.note: Build I frame, V(s)++, ack_pending=0. Author erratum 6621: "always want to restart T1 when an I frame is sent" — unconditional STOP_T3/START_T1
Drains mtu_out bytes, builds Iframe, vs++, emits SendIframe. TODO on Direwolf advice "always restart T1"
Window-bounded TX loop. Sends with P=1 only on last frame of window (308). Stops T3 / starts T1 at end (330-332). Handles t19-t22 uniformly
Drains TX_Q, sends from FRAMES[LINKNS], V(s)++. T1 reset to ONEMINUTE unconditionally — no distinction t19/t20

transitions.guard: not peer_receiver_busy and not V_s_eq_V_a_plus_k and not T1_running

references.quote: If timer T1 is not running, it is started. If timer T1 is running, it is restarted.

references.note: unconditional STOP_T3/START_T1 (covers t19 and t20)
if T1 not running: stop T3, start T1(srt)
same as t19 — no T1-state branch

transitions.notes: Window full (V(s) reached V(a)+k) — push back on queue and wait.

references.quote: The TNC does not transmit any more I frames if its send state variable equals the last received N(R) from the other side of the link plus k. If the TNC sent more I frames, the flow control window would be exceeded.

references.note: while-loop guard exits when window full (WITHIN_WINDOW_SIZE false)
vs == (va+k) mod modulus: window full, leaves obuf intact
Window check Outstanding >= LINKWINDOW; frames stay on TX_Q. No "push back" — never popped

transitions.notes: Peer busy — push back on queue.

references.quote: If a TNC is in a busy condition, it may still send I frames as long as the distant TNC is not also busy.

references.note: same loop guard handles peer_busy
Peer receiver busy → returns empty (no send)
PEER_RX_BUSY guard at line 260; loop skips
send loop guarded by POLLSENT not peer-busy; RNRSET consulted via timer policy

references.quote: AX.25 uses a special frame for this operation, the Unnumbered Information (UI) frame.

references.note: SOCK_DGRAM path: builds UI directly, bypasses LAPB state machine. Same path in any state

references.quote: Whenever a TNC enters a busy condition, it indicates this by sending an RNR response at the next opportunity.

references.note: RNR decided dynamically at supervisory-send time (QCOUNT<20 or session busy). No explicit DL-FLOW-OFF primitive
RNRSENT clears when no longer busy → SendSupervisCmd; closest analog to DL-FLOW-ON
RR(P) sent with PFBIT, L2TIMER restart via L2SENDCOMMAND

references.quote: If sent and received SABM(E) or DISC command frames are the same, both TNCs send a UA response at the earliest opportunity, and both devices enter the indicated state.

references.note: state_3/state_4 fall-through: discard_i_queue, establish_data_link, layer_3_initiated=1. Author enhancement at 1058-1060: state_5 if modulo==128 else state_1
Already STATE_3: rejects with -EISCONN. Linux does NOT implement re-establishment

references.quote: If sent and received SABM(E) or DISC command frames are the same, both TNCs send a UA response at the earliest opportunity, and both devices enter the indicated state.

references.note: modulo!=128 branch → state_1
default → ReleaseBuffer
default case silently no-ops

references.quote: When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.

references.note: default in S-frame switch → SDFRMR (FRMR state). NO Establish Data Link — sends FRMR instead

references.quote: When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.

references.note: same as t32 — no v22/v20 split

references.quote: When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.
When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.
When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.

references.note: Length<1 or >257 → drop. No DL-ERROR(N)

references.quote: When a TNC receives a frame with an incorrect FCS, an invalid frame, or a frame with an improper address, that frame is discarded.

references.note: same as t36

transitions.notes:
T1 expired — start the timer-recovery procedure. Note: figure
labels the subroutine "Transmit Enquery" (sic) — preserved
verbatim but the canonical verb here is the spec's
"Transmit Enquiry" routine.

references.quote: If the originating TNC's timer T1 expires while awaiting the distant TNC's acknowledgement of an I frame transmitted, the originating TNC restarts timer T1 and transmits an appropriate supervisory command frame (RR or RNR) with the P bit set.

references.note: State>=5: L2RETRIES++; sends DISC at N2 (3839) or SendSupervisCmd (3843). No separate TimerRecovery state in LinBPQ
state_3: SET_RC(1), transmit_enquiry, → state_4
Single method handles Connected (rc=1) and TimerRecovery (rc++). Author: "1998 spec doesn't say if it should be true or false. 2017 spec adds that pf should be false"
STATE_3: n2count=1, transmit_enquiry, → STATE_4

transitions.notes: T3 expired — long idle, poll the peer via Transmit Enquiry.

references.quote: T3 (inactive link timer): used to monitor data link traffic during idle periods to ensure the link is still functional.

references.note: L2SLOTIM (T3) decrement → SendSupervisCmd (RR(P) link-validation poll)
Author erratum 5633: "Original sets RC to 0, 2006 revision sets RC to 1 which makes more sense." Direwolf follows 2006
Author: "1998 bug: Says to set rc=0. Fixed in 2017." Sets rc=1, → TimerRecovery
n2count=0 then transmit_enquiry, → STATE_4. DIVERGES: figure says "stay in Connected"; Linux moves to TimerRecovery

transitions.notes:
Peer sent SABM while we're already Connected → reset to v2.0.
V(s) ≠ V(a) means we have unacknowledged data; discard queue and
notify L3 of the reset via DL-CONNECT indication.

references.quote: If the TNC receives a SABM(E) command while in the information-transfer state, it follows the resetting procedure outlined in Section 6.5.

references.note: If SESSACTIVE: InformPartner(NORMALCLOSE) + reset via L2SABM. LinBPQ uses SESSACTIVE flag instead of V(s)==V(a) — no DL-CONNECT-Indication
state_3/state_4 fall-through: UA F=p, clear_exception_conditions, error F, discard queue if vs!=va, reset V(), STOP_T1/START_T3
Sets version 2, delegates to sabm_or_sabme()
Combined SABM/SABME case 146-165. Collapses figure's t40-t43 — no per-F-bit or per-V-state branching

transitions.notes:
Peer SABM with V(s)==V(a) (no unacked data) — silent reset, no
DL-CONNECT indication to upper layer.

references.quote: If the TNC receives a SABM(E) command while in the information-transfer state, it follows the resetting procedure outlined in Section 6.5.

references.note: SESSACTIVE==0: repeat UA. Treats SABM as missed-UA case — no DL-CONNECT-Indication. Divergence from spec
vs!=va branch: discard I queue and re-issue server_link_established (figure's DL-CONNECT indication path)
Author: "2017 spec says to stop both T1 and T3 in state timer recovery. That can't be right, can it?" (only T1 stopped)

references.quote: If the TNC receives a SABM(E) command while in the information-transfer state, it follows the resetting procedure outlined in Section 6.5.

references.note: SABM and SABME merge in state_3. extended bool only routes set_version — structural collapse vs figc4.4
Sets version 2.2, delegates to sabm_or_sabme()
Same combined case as t40. Extended modulus set at 152-153

references.quote: If the TNC receives a SABM(E) command while in the information-transfer state, it follows the resetting procedure outlined in Section 6.5.

references.note: same as t41 — direwolf doesn't distinguish SABM/SABME branches
Author: "Added in 2017 spec, but only for Connected. TODO: should this be set also for TimerRecovery?" on rc=0 reset

references.quote: A TNC initiates a reset procedure whenever it receives an unexpected UA response frame, or after receipt of a FRMR frame from a TNC using an older version of the protocol.

references.note: FRMRHACK (L2Code.c:24) treats FRMR as DM — InformPartner(LINKLOST) + DM + CLEAROUTLINK. NOTABLE DIVERGENCE: spec says DL-ERROR(K)+Establish. Spec-compliant path commented out at 2192-2202
state_3/state_4: error K, set_version_2_0 (Erratum 5028: "FRMR can only be sent by v2.0"), establish_data_link, → state_1
Both Connected and TimerRecovery: layer3_initiated=false, DlError::K, establish_data_link, → AwaitingConnection
AX25_FRMR (and AX25_ILLEGAL) → establish_data_link + STATE_1. No DL-ERROR(K) report; no FRMR info bytes inspected

references.quote: A TNC initiates a reset procedure whenever it receives an unexpected UA response frame, or after receipt of a FRMR frame from a TNC using an older version of the protocol.

references.note: single path — direwolf has no separate L3-initiated branch
same handler as t44
same FRMRHACK path as t44 — no v22/v20 split
same case as t44

references.quote: A TNC initiates a reset procedure whenever it receives an unexpected UA response frame, or after receipt of a FRMR frame from a TNC using an older version of the protocol.

references.note: state_3/state_4: error C ("Unexpected UA"), establish_data_link, → state_1 or 5 (modulo author enhancement). Direwolf does NOT branch on F bit — figure's two-path F=1/F=0 collapsed
Author: "2017 spec says DlError::K, which is undocumented." Uses DlError::C, establish_data_link, → AwaitingConnection
UA in state 5 falls to default → ReleaseBuffer (silent ignore). NOTABLE DIVERGENCE from spec DL-ERROR(C)+Establish
No AX25_UA case in STATE_3 switch → silent default. DIVERGENCE: figure t46 calls for DL-ERROR(C)

references.quote: A TNC initiates a reset procedure whenever it receives an unexpected UA response frame, or after receipt of a FRMR frame from a TNC using an older version of the protocol.

references.note: same path as t46 — no F-bit branching
same handler as t46
same silent-ignore as t46
same default-case drop as t46. Linux uniform: no F-bit distinction

references.quote: AX.25 uses a special frame for this operation, the Unnumbered Information (UI) frame…Since this mode is connectionless, there are no requests for retransmissions of bad frames.

references.note: Only runs when cmd && pf==1. state_3/4 case calls enquiry_response with frame_type_U_UI
Calls Data::ui_check(cr, len); no enquiry response unless poll set
UI dispatched by PID (Netrom/IP/ARP). No UI_Check DL-error semantics — LinBPQ just routes
UI intercepted before LAPB state machine — handled state-independently

references.quote: AX.25 uses a special frame for this operation, the Unnumbered Information (UI) frame…

references.note: Poll branch: act.push(data.enquiry_response(true))
same path as t48 — direwolf only handles cmd && pf==1 case

transitions.notes:
Peer wants to disconnect — confirm with UA, notify L3, swap
timers and head to Disconnected. The figure draws stop_T3 then
start_T1 (and indeed wait for the peer's ack of the UA via T1).

references.quote: After receiving a valid DISC command, the TNC sends a UA response frame and enters the disconnected state.

references.note: InformPartner(NORMALCLOSE) + L2SENDUA + CLEAROUTLINK. Goes straight to Disconnected — no explicit T3-stop/T1-start
state_3/state_4: discard queue, UA F=p, server_link_terminated, STOP_T1, STOP_T3, → state_0
Author marks "Done." Clear queue, stop T1/T3, SendUa + EOF + Disconnected
UA F=pf, then ax25_disconnect(0) → STATE_0

transitions.notes:
Unexpected DM while connected — peer claims it's not connected.
DL-ERROR(E), tell L3 the link is gone, stop both timers.

references.note: InformPartner(LINKLOST) + CLEAROUTLINK. No explicit DL-ERROR(E)
state_3/state_4: error E, server_link_terminated, → state_0. Direwolf does NOT branch on F bit (spec figure shows F=1/F=0 paths)
Author marks "Done." DL-DISCONNECT, clear queue, stop T1/T3, DlError::E, → Disconnected
ax25_disconnect(ECONNRESET) → STATE_0. Linux ignores F bit (figure t51 only acts on F=1)

references.quote: Whenever an I or S frame is correctly received, even in a busy condition, the N(R) of the received frame is checked to see if it includes an acknowledgement of outstanding sent I frames.

references.note: treatasRR: clears RNRSET; ACKMSG releases acked frames. Stays in 5
state_3 entry. peer_receiver_busy=!ready. Erratum 3549-3552: check_need_for_response only for cmd&&pf (figure has unconditional)
Connected branch. Clears peer_receiver_busy, check_need_for_response, check_iframe_acked
Combined RR/RNR case. enquiry_response on cmd&&pf, then check_iframes_acked
SDREJF set by SDNRCHK → SDFRMR (FRMR state). NOTABLE DIVERGENCE: spec says N(r) Error Recovery → AwaitingConnection22. LinBPQ goes to FRMR state 3
bad N(R): nr_error_recovery, → state_1 (or state_5 modulo author enhancement)
N(R) out of window: nr_error_recovery → AwaitingConnection
On invalid N(R): nr_error_recovery + STATE_1
same SDFRMR path as t53 — no v22/v20 split
same as t53
same as t53
same as t53

references.quote: Whenever a TNC receives an RNR frame, it stops transmitting I frames until the busy condition is cleared.

references.note: Sets RNRSET (peer busy). RNR(F) restarts L2TIMER (2511) — explicit divergence comment at 2507
Same code path as RR; ready=0 sets peer_receiver_busy=1. Structural collapse RR/RNR
Shares RR/RNR case. Sets PEER_RX_BUSY (only difference from RR)

references.quote: Whenever a TNC receives an RNR frame, it stops transmitting I frames until the busy condition is cleared.

references.note: same SDFRMR path as t53
RR/RNR converge for bad-N(R)
same as t53
same SDFRMR path
same as t56
same as t53
state_3/4: i_frame_pop_off_queue, no enquiry_response (acknowledge_pending false). Erratum 2244: states 3 & 4 merged per 2006 revision
Same code path; acknowledge_pending check at 2235
Closest analog: delayed-ack timer (L2ACKREQ) fires SEND_RR_RESP. Not literally LM-SEIZE — deferred ack mechanism

references.quote: After receiving a SREJ frame, the transmitting TNC retransmits the individual I frame indicated by the N(R) contained in the SREJ at the next available opportunity.

references.note: SDNRCHK: SREJ without F doesn't ACK (early return)
state_3 entry. Erratum 4061-4067: figure has check_need_for_response but SREJ can only be response, so removed

references.quote: If the P bit was set, then all frames up to N(R)-1 are acknowledged.

references.note: SREJ(F) resend: clear POLLSENT, look up FRAMES[NS], resend. No V(s) advance / T3 restart — collapses spec's t60/t61
resend_for_srej. Author addition at 4082-4093: STOP_T3/START_T1 after sending I frame — avoids mutual-timeout deadlock
same SDFRMR path as t53
bad N(R): nr_error_recovery, → state_1 or 5. Erratum 4098: "Flow chart shows state 1 but that would not be appropriate if modulo is 128"
same as t62
same as t62

references.quote: After receiving a REJ frame, the transmitting TNC sets its send state variable to the same value as the REJ frame's received sequence number in the control field. The TNC then retransmits any I frame(s) outstanding at the next available opportunity.

references.note: REJ → POLLSENT check → RESETNS resets V(s) to N(r) (= Invoke_Retransmission). T1 cleared at 2486. No explicit Select_T1_Value
state_3: peer_receiver_busy=0, check_need_for_response UNCONDITIONAL (different from rr_rnr_frame), is_good_nr
AX25_REJ: clears PEER_RX_BUSY, enquiry_response on cmd&&pf, ax25_frames_acked + RTT + stop T1 + start T3 + requeue_frames on valid N(R)
same SDFRMR path
bad N(R): nr_error_recovery, → state_1 or 5 (author enhancement)
same as t53
same as t65
same as t65
same as t65

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and N_s_eq_V_r and V_r_I_frame_stored and P_eq_1
transitions.notes:
Loop body (retrieve / DL-DATA Indication / V(r)++) drained
via the `loop_while` step — iterates until V(r) I Frame Stored
returns false. Compiled to flat Actions[] with a LoopRange entry;
runtime loop semantics handled by loop-aware dispatchers, falls
back to one-iteration on legacy consumers (matches today's smoke
test behaviour).

references.quote: When an I frame is received…the frame is retained…This mode requires frame queuing and frame resequencing at the receiver.

references.note: Loop to drain stored out-of-order iframes — stub only (`while false`); TODO: "check for stored out of order frames"
Drain SREJ-buffered out-of-order frames now contiguous with V(R) — equivalent of figure's stored-frame loop
After REJ, when missing frame arrives, replays stored RXFRAMES via CheckNSLoop → PROC_I_FRAME at 2593

transitions.guard: command and info_field_valid and V_a_le_N_r_le_V_s and not own_receiver_busy and N_s_eq_V_r and V_r_I_frame_stored and not P_eq_1 and acknowledge_pending
transitions.notes:
Same stored-frame loop as t67; continues into the not-polled /
ack-pending tail.
